indentation-and-blocks.label: Indentation and Blocks
indentation-and-blocks.summary: Understand why indentation controls structure in Python.

quiz_1.title: What does indentation control in Python?
quiz_1.hint: Think about how Python interprets code structure.

help.concept: Indentation in Python defines the grouping of statements. It is crucial for creating blocks of code that belong together.
help.hint_1: Consider how other programming languages handle code blocks.
help.hint_2: Remember, indentation is not just for looks; it affects how the code runs.

options.d: It controls the structure of the code.

quiz_2.title: Which of the following are true about indentation in Python?
quiz_2.hint: Think about the consequences of incorrect indentation.

help.concept: Indentation affects how Python interprets the code, and incorrect indentation can lead to errors.
help.hint_1: Consider what happens if you mix tabs and spaces.
help.hint_2: Think about how indentation relates to control structures like loops and conditionals.

options.d: It can affect the logic of the program.

quiz_3.title: Complete the statement about indentation.
quiz_3.prompt: In Python, indentation is used to define a ________.
quiz_3.hint: Focus on the missing concept or keyword.

help.concept: This task checks the key idea behind complete the statement about indentation..
help.hint_1: Look for the word or phrase that best matches the lesson concept.
help.hint_2: Compare the blank with the meaning of each choice, not just the wording.

quiz_3.template: In Python, indentation is used to define a {blank}.

quiz_4.title: Create a simple conditional statement.
quiz_4.prompt: Write a program that checks if a number is positive, negative, or zero.
quiz_4.hint: Focus on the programming task being asked for, not on copying final solution text.

help.concept: Build the solution from the behavior the exercise is testing.
help.hint_1: Think about which steps, functions, or statements are required for the task.
help.hint_2: Construct the solution based on what result the exercise expects, not by repeating exact solution wording.

quiz_4.starterCode:
number = int(input('Enter a number: '))

sketch_1.title: Understanding Indentation
sketch_1.bodyMarkdown: In Python, indentation is not just for readability; it defines the structure of the code. Blocks of code are defined by their indentation level.

sketch_2.title: Why Indentation Matters
sketch_2.bodyMarkdown: Incorrect indentation can lead to errors or unexpected behavior in your program. Always ensure your code blocks are properly indented.
